videos.title: Most people get these 3 AI terms wrong
videos.description:
#AI hallucination is the flip side of creativity. That's the first of three terms most people misunderstand.

Term 1: Hallucination. The same mechanism that produces wrong answers is what generates creative writing, ideas, and novel connections. The real question is whether your task needs creativity or accuracy. Turning off web search in #ChatGPT or Claude gives you more creativity. Turning it on gives you more accuracy.

Term 2: Context window. Think of it as a fixed-size whiteboard. Once it's full, the AI drops older information to fit new input. If your AI starts giving weird answers mid-conversation, just start a new chat.

Term 3: Tokens. This is why free plans have usage limits. AI breaks your words into chunks called tokens, and every chunk costs usage. Better prompts mean fewer tokens, so you get more out of your plan before hitting the cap.

videos.title: Essentials: The Biology of Aggression, Mating & Arousal | Dr. David Anderson
videos.description:
In this Huberman Lab Essentials episode, my guest is Dr. David Anderson, PhD, a professor of biology at the California Institute of Technology (Caltech) and an investigator at the Howard Hughes Medical Institute (HHMI). We discuss the brain circuits that underlie how emotions emerge and shape behaviors, including the neural control of fear, aggression and pain. We also explore how hormones and neuromodulators influence these emotional states, and why understanding these hidden internal processes is essential for improving future mental health treatments.

Show notes: https://go.hubermanlab.com/blBjp0e

Watch more Huberman Lab Essentials: https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLPNW_gerXa4OGNy1yE-W9IX-tPu-tJa7S&si=a1_sA7rUT-fE0OM5

videos.title: If You Couldn't Fail, What Would You Do?
videos.description:
#Repost @milo_puzanoski

• • •If you knew you couldn’t fail, what would you go after right now?

That thing you always think about, but talk yourself out of.
The only difference between the people who make it and the ones who don’t is that they act anyway.
Because when you stop fearing failure, you become unstoppable.

DM me for editing inquiries @milo_puzanoski 👀

videos.title: Yash receives 100-150 Slack notifications daily, but only 30-40 require real action
videos.description: By categorizing messages into DMs, group mentions, threads, and channel mentions, then sub-categorizing into action-required, need-to-read, and FYI, he transformed anxiety-inducing notification overload into a manageable workflow.

videos.title: Build visual prototypes to communicate with cross-functional teams
videos.description: Yash's teammate used Perplexity Computer to prototype a persona-based redesign of Clay University by having it visually recognize the existing site and build a functional mockup. This created a "visual bridge" between stakeholders and designers, eliminating the communication gap that typically slows down design work.
